script_or_copy: Headline (top, white on dark gradient): 'जब एग्ज़ाम चले सिर्फ़ 14 दिन' (Jab exam chale sirf 14 din). Sub-line (white on magenta band): 'पूरे महीने का खर्चा क्यों?' (Poore mahine ka kharcha kyon?). Locked Wiom lockup (bottom, magenta footer): brand mark 'Wiom' + 'रिचार्ज वाला घर का नेट' (Recharge wala ghar ka net) + sub 'जितनी ज़रूरत, उतना रिचार्ज' (Jitni zaroorat, utna recharge — this is JZUR, NOT the locked JDRUDN rule) + pink house-with-WiFi icon.
visual_description: Real-feeling home interior. Foreground: mother (mid-late 30s, saree, no makeup, real-looking), hand to chin in a thoughtful/deciding pose, looking off-frame. Background mid-ground: daughter (looks ~9-11 years old, younger than the brief's 'teen') at a wooden dining table, on a laptop, with a steel cup beside her, studying. Setting: lived-in middle-class home — wall calendar on left (size of which makes the '2-week' marker unclear), kitchen visible through doorway, curtains, window light, simple shelving, a chair. Wiom logo pink on the magenta footer. Visual register: real, not aspirational; lighting is naturalistic.
notes: Produced version of S5 / JUN26-T-017 (Short-duration exam static, L2 Blocked-buyer, constraint-first, peer protagonist = mother-as-decider). NB: the lockup uses JZUR ('jitni zaroorat, utna recharge') in place of the locked memory anchor JDRUDN ('jitne din recharge, utne din net'). The S5 brief itself specified a JZUR-shaped articulation in the anchor position ('14 din ki zaroorat, 14 din ka recharge'), so the production follows that brief — but this is inconsistent with the wider doctrine that JDRUDN must appear verbatim. Evaluating G1/G2/Hero and the JDRUDN-absence question.
